title: Cryoprotection of mouse brain tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fho7j","text":"Fixed brains are submerged in 30% sucrose to cryoprotect prior to freezing and cutting cryosections.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"14c5c","text":"Following perfusion and post-fixation in 4% ice-cold PFA (for 2 hrs, overnight, or as appropriate for intended use):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6pjcd","text":"Dispose of fixative-contaminated sucrose and isopentane as hazardous waste or according to your intuitional guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Make 30% sucrose solution by dissolving 30 g of sucrose (BP220-1, Fisher) in 100 mL of 1X PBS (BP3991, Fisher) pH 7.4

Fill tube (20 mL glass scintillation vials or 15 mL conical centrifuge tubes are classically used) with ~ 10 mL of sucrose and transfer fixed brain tissue into tube, gently mix by inverting tube several times.

Prepare superchilled isopentane (2-methylbutane, M1246, Spectrum) by pouring ~ 30 mL into 50 ml bottle or beaker. Carefully position beaker on dry ice in a stable manner. Wait until isopentane temperature reaches -30 °C before proceeding to flash freezing.
Dry brain on paper towel by rolling it and gently pressing against paper towel.
Use long-handled forceps to transfer brain to superchilled isopentane. Let brain freeze for ~ 00:00:15 until completely white and bubbling stops.
Transfer to storage-tube that has been pre-labeled and superchilled on dry ice.
Transfer to -80 °C freezer for long-term storage.
creator: Lauren C. Faget
